name: Citrus Fruits

book_claims.claim: Vitamin C from citrus increases iron and zinc absorption from plant foods
book_claims.mechanism: Vitamin C reduces ferric iron (Fe3+) to ferrous iron (Fe2+) which is more readily absorbed; also chelates minerals making them more bioavailable
book_claims.study_ref: Refs 101, 102 in book (via lemon juice entry)

book_claims.claim: Provide antioxidant vitamins (especially vitamin C) that protect against oxidative damage
book_claims.mechanism: Vitamin C neutralises free radicals, prevents LDL oxidation

supplementary_research.finding: Aune et al. (2017, BMJ) meta-analysis of 95 prospective studies: citrus fruit consumption associated with reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, stroke, and total cancer in a dose-dependent manner.
Systematic review (Lv et al., 2015) of 14 prospective studies found citrus fruit consumption inversely associated with stroke risk, with a 10% risk reduction per 200g/day increment.
Hesperidin and naringenin (citrus flavanones) improve endothelial function, reduce blood pressure, and decrease inflammatory markers in human trials (Testai & Calderone, 2017).

culinary_pairings.source: Citrus dressings; vitamin C enhances iron absorption from greens
Classic pairing; acidity brightens and denatures proteins (ceviche)
Citrus vinaigrettes; fat improves flavanone absorption
Lemon on lentils/beans dramatically increases iron absorption
Ginger-citrus combinations in drinks and marinades
Lime-avocado pairing in guacamole; acidity balances fat

seasonality: Northern Hemisphere citrus peaks November-April. Lemons and limes available year-round. Blood oranges January-March. Grapefruit November-June. Southern Hemisphere offsets the season by six months.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: ~30-90 mg (oranges ~53 mg, lemons ~53 mg, grapefruit ~31 mg)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Nearly 100% bioavailable at typical dietary doses. Absorption decreases above ~200mg single dose.
Gut bacteria cleave to hesperetin for absorption. Improves endothelial function and reduces blood pressure.
Inhibits CYP3A4 enzyme -- caution with medications (statins, calcium channel blockers). Anti-inflammatory and insulin-sensitising.
Concentrated in zest/peel. Demonstrated anti-cancer properties in animal models; promotes phase II detoxification enzymes.
Pectin fermented to SCFAs by gut bacteria. Concentrated in pith and membranes -- eating whole segments preferred over juicing.

synergies.mechanism: Vitamin C increases non-heme iron absorption by 2-6x by reducing Fe3+ to Fe2+
Vitamin C stabilizes catechins in alkaline gut environment, improving absorption of EGCG by up to 6x
